Fort Worth Fire Deparment responded to a total of 7 structure fires during this MCS event: a commercial building on the 1100 block of North Sylvania Avenue, a residence in the 10300 block of Vintage Drive, a residence in the 1800 block of Marlene Drive, a residence in the 3800 block of Buckhorn Place, a residence in the 4500 block of East Loop 820, a residence at Wilbarger Street and Martin Luther King Freeway, and a residence at Shadydell Drive and Boat Club Road. A 3 foot diameter tree fell onto a house near College Avenue and Kilpatrick Lane. At the intersection of Whitney and Merritt, several large bradford pear trees and silver maple trees were knocked down. Other downed tree branches, uprooted trees, downed power lines, and downed fences were reported throughout Flower Mound. Part of the roof of City Garage at Princeton Street and Valley Parkway was torn off and landed on a nearby home. The Lewisville Visitor's Center also sustained roof damage and had to be closed. A traffic light pole was twisted around 90 degrees along Highway 114 1 mile northwest of Westlake. Peak winds of 59 mph were measured at the Fort Worth NWS Office. Peak winds of 70 mph were estimated in Southlake. Multiple tree limbs were down. Estimated winds of 60-65 mph were reported near North Tarrant Parkway and Davis Boulevard in North Richland Hills. Fence posts were snapped and tree limbs were down. Carports were destroyed at the Indigo Point Apartment complex. A 74 mph wind gust was measured near I-35E and State School Road in Denton. Most of the ceiling of a drive-through bank at Hebron Parkway and Old Denton Road in Carrollton was lost. Numerous trees were uprooted or knocked down in Coppell. Tree limbs were also knocked down. Many of the trees that were knocked over were bradford pears. Power outages also occurred. A large tree fell onto Jim's Cleaners on East Broad Street in Mansfield causing approximately $1,000 in damage to the roof. Significant damage occurred at Eagle Mountain Lake Marina. Most of the damage was to the docks, dock roofs, and walkways. Only one boat was lost and a few had scratches and dings. Eight teenagers were trapped during the storm and had to be lead out to safety. A home near Boat Club Road and Caballo Street was struck by lightning and sustained moderate damage. The lightning bolt entered through the roof and emerged through the laundry room. No possessions in the home were lost. A Ham radio operator measured 75 mph winds in Azle. Large tree limbs were knocked down around Azle. A COOP Observer measured 75 mph winds on Boat Club Road in Lake Worth.

Eight people were displaced from their home. Tree limbs that were 3-6 inches in diameter were broken off near the border of Flower Mound and Lewisville. Some service roads along I-35 in Denton were impassable and were being closed. Three feet of water was reported over the road at I-20 and Bryant Irvin Road. A recently built home on Centerboard Lane near Eagle Mountain Lake was completely destroyed in a fire started by lightning. All new appliances, furniture, and entertainment systems were lost. An EF0 tornado in Hebron was video taped by a storm chaser. This tornado occurred in a field south of SH 121 behind the Best Buy. The tornado occurred along the leading edge of the MCS, but video evidence suggests it was not a gustnado. A spotter measured a 63 mph wind gust in the Walnut Creek area in Mansfield. A 72 mph wind gust was measured at a Frisco Fire Station. Large trees were split in two and power lines were down in Frisco. A retired NWS employee reported a brief touchdown on FM 1171 3 miles west of FM 2499. The Fort Worth Spinks Airport (KFWS) AWOS measured a peak wind of 64 mph. The Villas at Waterchase apartment complex in Lewisville was hit by an EF-1 tornado. Sixteen units sustained some type of structural damage. Four chimneys were snapped off roofs on various buildings, a brick wall crumpled on one building and the roof of the same building was peeled back exposing the apartment homes. An EF-0 tornado traveled from Roanoke to Trophy Club. Storm spotters reported rotation and debris on the ground. A large bradford pear tree was snapped at a residence on Sublett Road in Arlington. A UPS tractor trailer was blown over onto an embankment on Highway 121 when strong crosswinds caused it to tip over. The AWOS in Grand Prairie measured a peak wind of 58 mph. The KDFW ASOS measured a peak wind of 64 mph. Fort Worth Fire Deparment responded to a total of 7 structure fires during this MCS event: a commercial building on the 1100 block of North Sylvania Avenue, a residence in the 10300 block of Vintage Drive, a residence in the 1800 block of Marlene Drive, a residence in the 3800 block of Buckhorn Place, a residence in the 4500 block of East Loop 820, a residence at Wilbarger Street and Martin Luther King Freeway, and a residence at Shadydell Drive and Boat Club Road. A 3 foot diameter tree fell onto a house near College Avenue and Kilpatrick Lane. At the intersection of Whitney and Merritt, several large bradford pear trees and silver maple trees were knocked down. Other downed tree branches, uprooted trees, downed power lines, and downed fences were reported throughout Flower Mound. Part of the roof of City Garage at Princeton Street and Valley Parkway was torn off and landed on a nearby home. The Lewisville Visitor's Center also sustained roof damage and had to be closed. A traffic light pole was twisted around 90 degrees along Highway 114 1 mile northwest of Westlake. Peak winds of 59 mph were measured at the Fort Worth NWS Office. Peak winds of 70 mph were estimated in Southlake. Multiple tree limbs were down. Estimated winds of 60-65 mph were reported near North Tarrant Parkway and Davis Boulevard in North Richland Hills. Fence posts were snapped and tree limbs were down. Carports were destroyed at the Indigo Point Apartment complex. A 74 mph wind gust was measured near I-35E and State School Road in Denton. Most of the ceiling of a drive-through bank at Hebron Parkway and Old Denton Road in Carrollton was lost. Numerous trees were uprooted or knocked down in Coppell. Tree limbs were also knocked down. Many of the trees that were knocked over were bradford pears. Power outages also occurred. A large tree fell onto Jim's Cleaners on East Broad Street in Mansfield causing approximately $1,000 in damage to the roof. Significant damage occurred at Eagle Mountain Lake Marina. Most of the damage was to the docks, dock roofs, and walkways. Only one boat was lost and a few had scratches and dings. Eight teenagers were trapped during the storm and had to be lead out to safety. A home near Boat Club Road and Caballo Street was struck by lightning and sustained moderate damage. The lightning bolt entered through the roof and emerged through the laundry room. No possessions in the home were lost. A Ham radio operator measured 75 mph winds in Azle. Large tree limbs were knocked down around Azle. A COOP Observer measured 75 mph winds on Boat Club Road in Lake Worth.
